ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a branded multi-component Singaporean snack platter. FOODON_03315091 (mixed snack food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed meat and fish snacks (processed, deep-fried)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for a multi-component Singaporean snack platter. A0CKM (mixed meat and fish snacks) is the closest defensible parent. Exact match not available.

serving.description: 1 party platter box (36 pieces total: 6 Cheezy Fish Finger, 4 Tiger Prawn, 6 Cheezy Chicken Ball, 6 Fishball, 6 Chicken Nugget, 8 Cheezy Chicken Sausage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One party platter box contains 36 assorted deep-fried snack pieces weighing approximately 720 g in total, intended for sharing among 4–6 people at a gathering or party.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient composition estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analogues: deep-fried fish finger (SR Legacy 15264), chicken nugget (SR Legacy 05062), fish ball (FAO/INFOODS SEA Table), chicken sausage (SR Legacy 07071), deep-fried prawn (SR Legacy 15150); weighted by piece count and estimated piece weight.
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this branded multi-component platter. Values are estimated by weighted average of USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA analogues for each component type (fish finger, prawn, chicken ball, fishball, chicken nugget, chicken sausage), proportioned by approximate piece count and estimated per-piece weight (~20 g each). Cheese-enriched variants assumed to add ~1.5 g fat and ~0.8 g protein per 100 g relative to plain analogues. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of deep-fried battered snack foods estimated from analogous breaded/battered fish and chicken products; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded platter. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogues for breaded/battered deep-fried fish products (GI ~50–60) and chicken nuggets/battered chicken (GI ~50–60), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The wheat-flour batter and breadcrumb coating are the primary glycemic drivers. GL per serving calculated as (55 × 125.3 g carbohydrate in full 720 g box) / 100 = 68.9, rounded to 69.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; estimated ~3744 mg per full box)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this platter due to its very high sodium load from processed fish paste, sausage, and seasoning; a single serving can exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 55) but with a very large carbohydrate load from wheat-flour batter and breadcrumbs across 36 pieces
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this platter as, despite its low glycemic index, the very high glycemic load (GL ~69 per box) from battered and breaded coatings can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; strict portion control to 4–6 pieces per sitting is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (13.2 g/100 g), saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), and very high sodium from deep-frying and processed meat components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this platter due to its combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated blood pressure; occasional small-portion consumption is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Party Platter Box (Classic Mix) is a branded multi-component deep-fried snack platter sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known Singaporean snack chain famous for its curry puffs and fried snacks. The box contains 36 pieces in total: 6 Cheezy Fish Fingers, 4 Tiger Prawns, 6 Cheezy Chicken Balls, 6 Fishballs, 6 Chicken Nuggets, and 8 Cheezy Chicken Sausages. All components are deep-fried and feature a golden, crispy battered or breadcrumbed exterior with a moist, savoury filling. The fish-based items (fish finger, fishball) are made from seasoned fish paste; the prawn items feature whole or minced tiger prawn; the chicken items (chicken ball, nugget, sausage) are made from minced chicken with cheese enrichment. The 'Cheezy' variants incorporate processed cheese, adding a mild dairy richness and slightly higher fat content. The platter is designed for sharing at parties, gatherings, and celebrations, and is typically served with chilli sauce or curry dipping sauce. Nutritionally, the platter is energy-dense (~228 kcal/100 g), high in sodium (~520 mg/100 g), and moderate in protein (~11.5 g/100 g) and fat (~13.2 g/100 g), with carbohydrates (~17.4 g/100 g) primarily from wheat-flour batter and breadcrumbs. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), and the glycemic load for the full box is high (~69). Clinically, this product is of concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load despite low GI), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, sodium). It is halal-certified and contains major allergens including gluten, fish, crustaceans, milk, and egg.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific branded multi-component platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted average of USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA analogues for each component type. GI is estimated from analogous breaded/battered products in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component analogues are well-established in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA component analogues weighted by piece count and estimated piece weight (~20 g per piece, 36 pieces total = ~720 g). No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this product. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogues used for estimation. 3. The 'Cheezy' variants (Fish Finger, Chicken Ball, Chicken Sausage) are assumed to add approximately 1.5 g fat and 0.8 g protein per 100 g relative to plain analogues, based on typical processed cheese addition levels. 4. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore). 5. Price SGD 26.80 preserved from input source (field survey data, 2024). 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Serving size of 720 g is an estimate based on 36 pieces at ~20 g each; actual weight may vary. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component analogues and should be treated as approximate. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component analogues used for this estimation.
